mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals accelerating manufacturing output and economic expansion, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the average 20-day forward return in months with rising INDPRO is not significantly greater than zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.0% indicates labor market weakness, reducing consumer income and spending, which depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Zero or positive average 5-day forward return over all UNRATE > 5.0 observations, or less than 10 triggered events in the backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and stable while core inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises moderately, real interest rates remain suppressed, encouraging equity investment and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction-term coefficient in a linear regression of 20-day forward returns on FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) or the model out-of-sample R-squared is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in retail sales (RSAFS) over three months signals a consumer spending contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days, but this effect only holds during late-cycle expansion regimes when consumer leverage is high; in early recovery the same decline may be positive if driven by precautionary saving.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Conditional on the regime being late-cycle, average 60-day forward return after trigger is not negative, or the regime classification (based on ISM manufacturing PMI durables) cannot be replicated with macro data available at the decision point.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units reflects deteriorating residential investment and consumer confidence, foreshadowing economic weakness that weighs on equity markets and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return after HOUST < 1200 is non-negative over a 10-year walk-forward, or the trigger fires fewer than 10 times.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) accelerates while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously declines, the labor market tightens, boosting wage growth and aggregate demand, but also raising inflation fears that can lead to Federal Reserve tightening; the net effect is a negative S&P 500 return over 60 trading days as monetary tightening concerns dominate.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not produce a negative average 60-day forward return that is statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over the out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained modest inflation (CPIAUCSL) near the Federal Reserve’s 2% target supports nominal revenue growth for corporations and prevents deflationary debt dynamics, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between CPIAUCSL level and 20-day forward SPX return is non-positive over a 10-year rolling window, or the average return in months with CPIAUCSL between 1.5% and 2.5% is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt cut in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) by the Federal Reserve signals emergency monetary easing, which initially boosts equity markets, but over a 5-day horizon the panic that prompted the cut dominates, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by at least 25bp in a single meeting
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return after an emergency cut is non-negative, or the identification of emergency cuts (e.g., inter-meeting moves) fails to produce at least 5 out-of-sample events.
